req.title: Create observation (audit)
req.statement: A clinician shall create an Observation tied to a patient with LOINC-coded code element; Observations without code shall be rejected. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
# pytest Tests: Observation Creation with LOINC Code + AuditEvent End-to-End

```python
"""
Test Suite: Observation Creation with LOINC Code Requirement
Requirement: A clinician shall create an Observation tied to a patient with
             LOINC-coded code element; Observations without code shall be
             rejected. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
             and tested end-to-end.

Assumptions / conventions:
  - FHIR R4 REST API under test (configurable via ENV / fixtures)
  - pytest + requests
  - A real or stub FHIR server (e.g. HAPI FHIR, Medplum) is reachable
  - AuditEvents are written to the same FHIR server and queryable
  - Auth token is provided via fixture (Bearer JWT)
"""

import time
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Generator

import pytest
import requests

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Constants
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
LOINC_SYSTEM = "http://loinc.org"
SNOMED_SYSTEM = "http://snomed.info/sct"
INVALID_SYSTEM = "http://example.com/made-up-codes"

HEART_RATE_LOINC = "8867-4"       # Heart rate
BODY_WEIGHT_LOINC = "29463-7"     # Body weight
BLOOD_PRESSURE_LOINC = "55284-4"  # Blood pressure panel

AUDIT_POLL_RETRIES = 10
AUDIT_POLL_INTERVAL_S = 0.5       # seconds between retries


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers / builders
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _loinc_code(code: str, display: str) -> dict:
    return {
        "coding": [
            {
                "system": LOINC_SYSTEM,
                "code": code,
                "display": display,
            }
        ]
    }


def _minimal_observation(patient_ref: str, code: dict | None = None) -> dict:
    """Build a minimal valid Observation resource."""
    obs: dict = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "subject": {"reference": patient_ref},
        "effectiveDateTime": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        "valueQuantity": {
            "value": 72,
            "unit": "beats/minute",
            "system": "http://unitsofmeasure.org",
            "code": "/min",
        },
    }
    if code is not None:
        obs["code"] = code
    return obs


def _wait_for_audit_event(
    session: requests.Session,
    base_url: str,
    entity_reference: str,
    action: str | None = None,
) -> dict | None:
    """
    Poll the FHIR server for an AuditEvent whose entity.what.reference
    matches the given resource reference.  Returns the first matching
    AuditEvent resource or None.
    """
    params = {"entity": entity_reference}
    if action:
        params["action"] = action

    for _ in range(AUDIT_POLL_RETRIES):
        resp = session.get(f"{base_url}/AuditEvent", params=params)
        resp.raise_for_status()
        bundle = resp.json()
        entries = bundle.get("entry", [])
        if entries:
            return entries[0]["resource"]
        time.sleep(AUDIT_POLL_INTERVAL_S)
    return None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def fhir_base_url(pytestconfig) -> str:
    return pytestconfig.getoption("--fhir-base-url", default="http://localhost:8080/fhir")


@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def auth_token(pytestconfig) -> str:
    """
    Returns a Bearer token for a clinician role.
    Override via --auth-token CLI option or supply a real token.
    """
    return pytestconfig.getoption("--auth-token", default="test-clinician-token")


@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def session(auth_token: str) -> requests.Session:
    s = requests.Session()
    s.headers.update(
        {
            "Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}",
            "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json",
            "Accept": "application/fhir+json",
        }
    )
    return s


@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def test_patient(session: requests.Session, fhir_base_url: str) -> dict:
    """Create a Patient resource once for the test session."""
    patient = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "identifier": [
            {
                "system": "http://example.com/test-patients",
                "value": f"test-{uuid.uuid4()}",
            }
        ],
        "name": [{"family": "TestPatient", "given": ["Fixture"]}],
        "gender": "unknown",
        "birthDate": "1980-01-01",
    }
    resp = session.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/Patient", json=patient)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Could not create test patient: {resp.text}"
    return resp.json()


@pytest.fixture
def patient_reference(test_patient: dict) -> str:
    patient_id = test_patient["id"]
    return f"Patient/{patient_id}"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Register custom CLI options
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def pytest_addoption(parser):
    parser.addoption("--fhir-base-url", action="store", default="http://localhost:8080/fhir")
    parser.addoption("--auth-token", action="store", default="test-clinician-token")


# ===========================================================================
# TEST CLASS 1 – Successful Observation creation with LOINC code
# ===========================================================================

class TestObservationCreationSuccess:
    """Happy-path tests: clinician creates Observations with valid LOINC codes."""

    def test_create_observation_with_loinc_returns_201(
        self, session, fhir_base_url, patient_reference
    ):
        """A POST with a LOINC-coded Observation must return HTTP 201 Created."""
        obs = _minimal_observation(
            patient_reference,
            code=_loinc_code(HEART_RATE_LOINC, "Heart rate"),
        )
        resp = session.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation", json=obs)

        assert resp.status_code == 201, (
            f"Expected 201 Created, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
        )

    def test_create_observation_response_contains_id(
        self, session, fhir_base_url, patient_reference
    ):
        """Server must assign an id to the created Observation."""
        obs = _minimal_observation(
            patient_reference,
            code=_loinc_code(HEART_RATE_LOINC, "Heart rate"),
        )
        resp = session.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation", json=obs)
        body = resp.json()

        assert "id" in body, "Response body must contain 'id'"
        assert body["id"], "id must be non-empty"

    def test_created_observation_links_to_patient(
        self, session, fhir_base_url, patient_reference
    ):
        """The persisted Observation must reference the correct Patient."""
        obs = _minimal_observation(
            patient_reference,
            code=_loinc_code(HEART_RATE_LOINC, "Heart rate"),
        )
        resp = session.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation", json=obs)
        created = resp.json()

        obs_id = created["id"]
        get_resp = session.get(f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation/{obs_id}")
        assert get_resp.status_code == 200
        retrieved = get_resp.json()

        assert retrieved["subject"]["reference"] == patient_reference, (
            "Observation subject must reference the creating patient"
        )

    def test_created_observation_preserves_loinc_coding(
        self, session, fhir_base_url, patient_reference
    ):
        """The persisted Observation must preserve the LOINC system and code."""
        obs = _minimal_observation(
            patient_reference,
            code=_loinc_code(BODY_WEIGHT_LOINC, "Body weight"),
        )
        resp = session.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation", json=obs)
        obs_id = resp.json()["id"]

        retrieved = session.get(f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation/{obs_id}").json()
        codings = retrieved["code"]["coding"]

        loinc_codings = [c for c in codings if c.get("system") == LOINC_SYSTEM]
        assert loinc_codings, "No LOINC coding found in persisted Observation"
        assert loinc_codings[0]["code"] == BODY_WEIGHT_LOINC

    def test_create_multiple_loinc_observations_for_same_patient(
        self, session, fhir_base_url, patient_reference
    ):
        """Multiple Observations with different LOINC codes can be created for one patient."""
        codes = [
            (HEART_RATE_LOINC, "Heart rate"),
            (BODY_WEIGHT_LOINC, "Body weight"),
            (BLOOD_PRESSURE_LOINC, "Blood pressure"),
        ]
        created_ids = []
        for code, display in codes:
            obs = _minimal_observation(
                patient_reference,
                code=_loinc_code(code, display),
            )
            resp = session.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation", json=obs)
            assert resp.status_code == 201, (
                f"Failed for code {code}: {resp.text}"
            )
            created_ids.append(resp.json()["id"])

        assert len(set(created_ids)) == 3, "Each Observation must receive a unique id"

    def test_search_observations_by_patient_and_loinc_code(
        self, session, fhir_base_url, patient_reference, test_patient
    ):
        """Created Observation must be findable by patient + LOINC code search."""
        obs = _minimal_observation(
            patient_reference,
            code=_loinc_code(HEART_RATE_LOINC, "Heart rate"),
        )
        session.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation", json=obs)

        patient_id = test_patient["id"]
        search_resp = session.get(
            f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation",
            params={
                "subject": patient_id,
                "code": f"{LOINC_SYSTEM}|{HEART_RATE_LOINC}",
            },
        )
        assert search_resp.status_code == 200
        bundle = search_resp.json()
        assert bundle.get("total", 0) >= 1, (
            "At least one matching Observation expected in search results"
        )


# ===========================================================================
# TEST CLASS 2 – Rejection of Observations without a code element
# ===========================================================================

class TestObservationRejectionNoCode:
    """Negative tests: server must reject Observations missing the code element."""

    def test_observation_without_code_returns_4xx(
        self, session, fhir_base_url, patient_reference
    ):
        """POST of an Observation with no code element must be rejected (4xx)."""
        obs = _minimal_observation(patient_reference, code=None)  # no code
        resp = session.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation", json=obs)

        assert 400 <= resp.status_code < 500, (
            f"Expected 4xx for missing code, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
        )

    def test_observation_without_code_returns_operation_outcome(
        self, session, fhir_base_url, patient_reference
    ):
        """Rejection response must be a FHIR OperationOutcome resource."""
        obs = _minimal_observation(patient_reference, code=None)
        resp = session.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation", json=obs)
        body = resp.json()

        assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome", (
            f"Expected OperationOutcome, got {body.get('resourceType')}"
        )

    def test_observation_without_code_operation_outcome_has_error_issue(
        self, session, fhir_base_url, patient_reference
    ):
        """OperationOutcome must contain at least one issue with severity 'error' or 'fatal'."""
        obs = _minimal_observation(patient_reference, code=None)
        resp = session.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation", json=obs)
        issues = resp.json().get("issue", [])

        error_issues = [
            i for i in issues if i.get("severity") in ("error", "fatal")
        ]
        assert error_issues, "OperationOutcome must have at least one error/fatal issue"

    def test_observation_with_empty_code_object_is_rejected(
        self, session, fhir_base_url, patient_reference
    ):
        """An Observation with code: {} (empty code) must also be rejected."""
        obs = _minimal_observation(patient_reference, code={})
        resp = session.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation", json=obs)

        assert 400 <= resp.status_code < 500, (
            f"Expected 4xx for empty code object, got {resp.status_code}"
        )

    def test_observation_with_empty_coding_array_is_rejected(
        self, session, fhir_base_url, patient_reference
    ):
        """An Observation with code.coding = [] must be rejected."""
        obs = _minimal_observation(patient_reference, code={"coding": []})
        resp = session.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation", json=obs)

        assert 400 <= resp.status_code < 500, (
            f"Expected 4xx for empty coding array, got {resp.status_code}"
        )

    def test_observation_with_non_loinc_code_system_is_rejected(
        self, session, fhir_base_url, patient_reference
    ):
        """
        An Observation coded with a non-LOINC system must be rejected when
        the business rule mandates LOINC.
        """
        non_loinc_code = {
            "coding": [
                {
                    "system": INVALID_SYSTEM,
                    "code": "ABC-123",
                    "display": "Made-up code",
                }
            ]
        }
        obs = _minimal_observation(patient_reference, code=non_loinc_code)
        resp = session.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation", json=obs)

        assert 400 <= resp.status_code < 500, (
            f"Expected 4xx for non-LOINC system, got {resp.status_code}"
        )

    def test_observation_with_snomed_only_code_is_rejected(
        self, session, fhir_base_url, patient_reference
    ):
        """SNOMED-only coding must be rejected when LOINC is required."""
        snomed_code = {
            "coding": [
                {
                    "system": SNOMED_SYSTEM,
                    "code": "364075005",
                    "display": "Heart rate",
                }
            ]
        }
        obs = _minimal_observation(patient_reference, code=snomed_code)
        resp = session.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation", json=obs)

        assert 400 <= resp.status_code < 500, (
            f"Expected 4xx for SNOMED-only code, got {resp.status_code}"
        )

    def test_observation_missing_code_is_not_persisted(
        self, session, fhir_base_url, patient_reference, test_patient
    ):
        """A rejected Observation must NOT be retrievable via search."""
        identifier_value = f"reject-test-{uuid.uuid4()}"
        obs = _minimal_observation(patient_reference, code=None)
        obs["identifier"] = [
            {"system": "http://example.com/test", "value": identifier_value}
        ]

        session.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation", json=obs)

        search_resp = session.get(
            f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation",
            params={"identifier": f"http://example.com/test|{identifier_value}"},
        )
        bundle = search_resp.json()
        assert bundle.get("total", 0) == 0, (
            "Rejected Observation must not be persisted on the server"
        )


# ===========================================================================
# TEST CLASS 3 – AuditEvent emission (end-to-end)
# ===========================================================================

class TestAuditEventEmission:
    """End-to-end tests: every Observation operation must emit an AuditEvent."""

    def test_successful_observation_creation_emits_audit_event(
        self, session, fhir_base_url, patient_reference
    ):
        """Creating a valid Observation must produce an AuditEvent referencing it."""
        obs = _minimal_observation(
            patient_reference,
            code=_loinc_code(HEART_RATE_LOINC, "Heart rate"),
        )
        resp = session.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation", json=obs)
        assert resp.status_code == 201
        obs_id = resp.json()["id"]

        audit = _wait_for_audit_event(
            session, fhir_base_url, f"Observation/{obs_id}"
        )
        assert audit is not None, (
            f"No AuditEvent found for Observation/{obs_id} after "
            f"{AUDIT_POLL_RETRIES * AUDIT_POLL_INTERVAL_S}s"
        )

    def test_audit_event_action_is_create_for_post(
        self, session, fhir_base_url, patient_reference
    ):
        """AuditEvent.action must be 'C' (Create) for a POST operation."""
        obs = _minimal_observation(
            patient_reference,
            code=_loinc_code(HEART_RATE_LOINC, "Heart rate"),
        )
        resp = session.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation", json=obs)
        obs_id = resp.json()["id"]

        audit = _wait_for_audit_event(
            session, fhir_base_url, f"Observation/{obs_id}", action="C"
        )
        assert audit is not None, "AuditEvent with action='C' not found"
        assert audit.get("action") == "C", (
            f"Expected action 'C', got '{audit.get('action')}'"
        )

    def test_audit_event_outcome_success_for_valid_observation(
        self, session, fhir_base_url, patient_reference
    ):
        """AuditEvent.outcome must indicate success (0) for a valid creation."""
        obs = _minimal_observation(
            patient_reference,
            code=_loinc_code(HEART_RATE_LOINC, "Heart rate"),
        )
        resp = session.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation", json=obs)
        obs_id = resp.json()["id"]

        audit = _wait_for_audit_event(
            session, fhir_base_url, f"Observation/{obs_id}"
        )
        assert audit is not None
        # FHIR AuditEvent.outcome: "0" = success
        assert audit.get("outcome") in ("0", 0, None), (
            f"Expected success outcome, got '{audit.get('outcome')}'"
        )

    def test_audit_event_records_agent_clinician(
        self, session, fhir_base_url, patient_reference, auth_token
    ):
        """AuditEvent must record an agent representing the acting clinician."""
        obs = _minimal_observation(
            patient_reference,
            code=_loinc_code(HEART_RATE_LOINC, "Heart rate"),
        )
        resp = session.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation", json=obs)
        obs_id = resp.json()["id"]

        audit = _wait_for_audit_event(
            session, fhir_base_url, f"Observation/{obs_id}"
        )
        assert audit is not None
        agents = audit.get("agent", [])
        assert agents, "AuditEvent must have at least one agent"

        # At least one agent must be marked as the requestor
        requestors = [a for a in agents if a.get("requestor") is True]
        assert requestors, "AuditEvent must have an agent with requestor=true"

    def test_audit_event_entity_references_created_observation(
        self, session, fhir_base_url, patient_reference
    ):
        """AuditEvent.entity must reference the newly created Observation."""
        obs = _minimal_observation(
            patient_reference,
            code=_loinc_code(BODY_WEIGHT_LOINC, "Body weight"),
        )
        resp = session.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation", json=obs)
        obs_id = resp.json()["id"]
        expected_ref = f"Observation/{obs_id}"

        audit = _wait_for_audit_event(session, fhir_base_url, expected_ref)
        assert audit is not None

        entities = audit.get("entity", [])
        entity_refs = [
            e.get("what", {}).get("reference") for e in entities
        ]
        assert expected_ref in entity_refs, (
            f"Expected entity reference '{expected_ref}' in {entity_refs}"
        )

    def test_rejected_observation_also_emits_audit_event(
        self, session, fhir_base_url, patient_reference
    ):
        """
        Even a rejected (no-code) Observation attempt must produce an
        AuditEvent with a non-success outcome.
        """
        # Tag the request with a unique identifier so we can locate its audit event
        request_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        obs = _minimal_observation(patient_reference, code=None)

        reject_resp = session.post(
            f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation",
            json=obs,
            headers={"X-Request-Id": request_id},
        )
        assert 400 <= reject_resp.status_code < 500

        # Query by X-Request-Id correlation or by outcome != 0
        # Implementation note: exact query param depends on server capability;
        # here we search for recent failure AuditEvents and match by correlationId
        params = {
            "outcomeDesc": "code",          # partial description match
            "action": "C",
            "_sort": "-date",
            "_count": "20",
        }
        failure_audit = None
        for _ in range(AUDIT_POLL_RETRIES):
            resp = session.get(f"{fhir_base_url}/AuditEvent", params=params)
            resp.raise_for_status()
            entries = resp.json().get("entry", [])
            for entry in entries:
                resource = entry["resource"]
                # Match by outcome != 0 (FHIR: "4"=minor failure, "8"=serious, "12"=major)
                if resource.get("outcome") not in ("0", 0, None):
                    failure_audit = resource
                    break
            if failure_audit:
                break
            time.sleep(AUDIT_POLL_INTERVAL_S)

        assert failure_audit is not None, (
            "An AuditEvent with non-success outcome must be emitted for rejected Observations"
        )
        assert failure_audit.get("outcome") not in ("0", 0, None), (
            "AuditEvent outcome must indicate failure for rejected request"
        )

    def test_audit_event_has_recorded_timestamp(
        self, session, fhir_base_url, patient_reference
    ):
        """AuditEvent.recorded must be a valid ISO-8601 datetime."""
        obs = _minimal_observation(
            patient_reference,
            code=_loinc_code(HEART_RATE_LOINC, "Heart rate"),
        )
        resp = session.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation", json=obs)
        obs_id = resp.json()["id"]

        audit = _wait_for_audit_event(
            session, fhir_base_url, f"Observation/{obs_id}"
        )
        assert audit is not None
        recorded = audit.get("recorded")
        assert recorded, "AuditEvent.recorded must be present"

        # Must parse as ISO-8601 datetime
        try:
            datetime.fromisoformat(recorded.replace("Z", "+00:00"))
        except ValueError as exc:
            pytest.fail(f"AuditEvent.recorded is not a valid datetime: {recorded!r} ({exc})")

    def test_audit_event_type_indicates_rest_operation(
        self, session, fhir_base_url, patient_reference
    ):
        """AuditEvent.type should indicate a REST operation (DICOM code 110114)."""
        obs = _minimal_observation(
            patient_reference,
            code=_loinc_code(HEART_RATE_LOINC, "Heart rate"),
        )
        resp = session.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation", json=obs)
        obs_id = resp.json()["id"]

        audit = _wait_for_audit_event(
            session, fhir_base_url, f"Observation/{obs_id}"
        )
        assert audit is not None
        audit_type = audit.get("type", {})
        coding = audit_type.get("code", "")
        # DICOM 110114 = "User Authentication" or REST; many servers use this
        assert coding, "AuditEvent.type.code must be present"

    def test_read_observation_emits_audit_event_with_action_r(
        self, session, fhir_base_url, patient_reference
    ):
        """Reading an Observation (GET) must emit an AuditEvent with action='R'."""
        # Create first
        obs = _minimal_observation(
            patient_reference,
            code=_loinc_code(HEART_RATE_LOINC, "Heart rate"),
        )
        create_resp = session.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation", json=obs)
        obs_id = create_resp.json()["id"]

        # Read the observation
        read_resp = session.get(f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation/{obs_id}")
        assert read_resp.status_code == 200

        audit = _wait_for_audit_event(
            session, fhir_base_url, f"Observation/{obs_id}", action="R"
        )
        assert audit is not None, (
            f"No AuditEvent with action='R' found for Observation/{obs_id}"
        )
        assert audit.get("action") == "R"

    def test_update_observation_emits_audit_event_with_action_u(
        self, session, fhir_base_url, patient_reference
    ):
        """Updating an Observation (PUT) must emit an AuditEvent with action='U'."""
        obs = _minimal_observation(
            patient_reference,
            code=_loinc_code(HEART_RATE_LOINC, "Heart rate"),
        )
        create_resp = session.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation", json=obs)
        obs_id = create_resp.json()["id"]
        obs_version = create_resp.json().get("meta", {}).get("versionId", "1")

        # Update: change status to amended
        updated_obs = create_resp.json()
        updated_obs["status"] = "amended"
        update_resp = session.put(
            f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation/{obs_id}",
            json=updated_obs,
            headers={"If-Match": f'W/"{obs_version}"'},
        )
        assert update_resp.status_code in (200, 201)

        audit = _wait_for_audit_event(
            session, fhir_base_url, f"Observation/{obs_id}", action="U"
        )
        assert audit is not None, (
            f"No AuditEvent with action='U' found for Observation/{obs_id}"
        )
        assert audit.get("action") == "U"

    def test_delete_observation_emits_audit_event_with_action_d(
        self, session, fhir_base_url, patient_reference
    ):
        """Deleting an Observation (DELETE) must emit an AuditEvent with action='D'."""
        obs = _minimal_observation(
            patient_reference,
            code=_loinc_code(HEART_RATE_LOINC, "Heart rate"),
        )
        create_resp = session.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation", json=obs)
        obs_id = create_resp.json()["id"]

        delete_resp = session.delete(f"{fhir_base_url}/Observation/{obs_id}")
        assert delete_resp.status_code in (200, 204)

        audit = _wait_for_audit_event(
            session, fhir_base_url, f"Observation/{obs_id}", action="D"
        )
        assert audit is not None, (
            f"No AuditEvent with action='D' found for Observation/{obs_id}"
        )
        assert audit.get("action") == "D"


# ===========================================================================
# TEST CLASS 4 – Parametrised / edge-case matrix
# ===========================================================================

class TestObservationEdgeCases:
    """Parametrised edge-case coverage."""

    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "code_payload, expected_status_range",
        [
            # Valid LOINC codes
            (
                _loinc_code(HEART_RATE_LOINC, "Heart rate"),
                (201, 201),
            ),
            (
                _loinc_code(BODY_WEIGHT_LOINC, "Body weight"),
                (201, 201),
            ),
            # Missing code
            (None, (400, 422)),
            # Empty code dict
            ({}, (400, 422)),
            # Wrong system
            (
                {
                    "coding": [
                        {"system": INVALID_SYSTEM, "code": "XYZ", "display": "Bad"}
                    ]
                },
                (400, 422),
            ),
        ],

# pytest Tests: Observation Creation with LOINC Code + AuditEvent End-to-End

```python
"""
Test Suite: Observation Creation with LOINC Code Requirement
Requirement: A clinician shall create an Observation tied to a patient with
             LOINC-coded code element; Observations without code shall be
             rejected. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
             and tested end-to-end.

Assumptions / conventions:
  - FHIR R4 REST API under test (configurable via ENV / fixtures)
  - pytest + requests
  - A real or stub FHIR server (e.g. HAPI FHIR, Medplum) is reachable
  - AuditEvents are written to the same FHIR server and queryable
  - Auth token is provided via fixture (Bearer JWT)
"""

import time
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Generator

import pytest
import requests

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Constants
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
LOINC_SYSTEM = "http://loinc.org"
SNOMED_SYSTEM = "http://snomed.info/sct"
INVALID_SYSTEM = "http://example.com/made-up-codes"

HEART_RATE_LOINC = "8867-4"       # Heart rate
BODY_WEIGHT_LOINC = "29463-7"     # Body weight
BLOOD_PRESSURE_LOINC = "55284-4"  # Blood pressure panel

AUDIT_POLL_RETRIES = 10
AUDIT_POLL_INTERVAL_S = 0.5       # seconds between retries


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers / builders
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _loinc_code(code: str, display: str) -> dict:
    return {
        "coding": [
            {
                "system": LOINC_SYSTEM,
                "code": code,
                "display": display,
            }
        ]
    }


def _minimal_observation(patient_ref: str, code: dict | None = None) -> dict:
    """Build a minimal valid Observation resource."""
    obs: dict = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "subject": {"reference": patient_ref},
        "effectiveDateTime": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        "valueQuantity": {
            "value": 72,
            "unit": "beats/minute",
            "system": "http://unitsofmeasure.org",
            "code": "/min",
        },
    }
    if code is not None:
        obs["code"] = code
    return obs


def _wait_for_audit_event(
    session: requests.Session,
    base_url: str,
    entity_reference: str,
    action: str | None = None,
) -> dict | None:
    """
    Poll the FHIR server for an AuditEvent whose entity.what.reference
    matches the given resource reference.  Returns the first matching
    AuditEvent resource or None.
    """
    params = {"entity": entity_reference}
    if action:
        params["action"] = action

    for _ in range(AUDIT_POLL_RETRIES):
        resp = session.get(f"{base_url}/AuditEvent", params=params)
        resp.raise_for_status()
        bundle = resp.json()
        entries = bundle.get("entry", [])
        if entries:
            return entries[0]["resource"]
        time.sleep(AUDIT_POLL_INTERVAL_S)
    return None

# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def fhir_base_url(pytestconfig) -> str:
    return pytestconfig.getoption("--fhir-base-url", default="http://localhost:8080/fhir")


@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def auth_token(pytestconfig) -> str:
    """
    Returns a Bearer token for a clinician role.
    Override via --auth-token CLI option or supply a real token.
    """
    return pytestconfig.getoption("--auth-token", default="test-clinician-token")


@pytest.fixture(scope="session")

    s = requests.Session()
    s.headers.update(
        {
            "Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}",
            "Content-Type": "application
